text.title: Trend Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Trend Analysis methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Trend score MUST aggregate >=5 signal classes (search, papers, funding, GitHub stars, social).
rule.rationale: Single-source signals over-fit to one community's hype.

rule.statement: Composite trend score MUST be on a 0-100 scale, not 1-5, so deltas across trends are comparable.
rule.rationale: 1-5 scales saturate too fast for cross-trend ranking.

rule.statement: Every trend MUST carry a 4-quarter decay-curve estimate (signal Q+1, Q+2, Q+3, Q+4).
rule.rationale: Trends without decay estimates cannot inform bet/monitor/ignore timing.

rule.statement: Trend MUST be flagged hype | structural based on decay curve slope.
rule.rationale: Hype cycles repay early-mover; structural trends repay patience.

rule.statement: Every signal value MUST cite the source + retrieval date.
rule.rationale: Re-running the analysis next quarter needs reproducibility.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
